You can grab the newest and smallest 23L model down at $617 shipped (sitting $32 under Amazon’s pricing), the middle-range 40L model at $664.99 shipped (sitting $10 under Amazon), or the largest 58L dual-zone model at $807.49 shipped (sitting $42 under Amazon) – plus, they all come with a FREE Road Trip Kit ($60 value). While they may carry $799, $900, and $1,100 MSRPs, we’ve only seen them climb as high as $700, $820, and $1,000 in 2026. While we have seen them go a bit lower in the past, tariffs continue to make the market uncertain, causing less discounts – so these combined $83, $155, and $193 markdowns off their 2026 going rates are solid deals that beat out all the other prices we’ve seen since Black Friday. You won’t find these models available on Amazon just yet, so jumping on these deals is the best way to maximize savings on the latest commuting tech the brand has to offer.
